values.content:
You are a senior business analyst. From the company data below, output structured JSON.

SECTION 1 — company_profile
- company: exact name as provided
- industry: infer from businessDescription
- country: infer from website domain (.co.uk=UK, .de=Germany, .com=US default)
- primaryFocus: one sentence on what the company does
- keyPriorities: 3–5 strategic priorities for this size and industry
- employees: range midpoint (1-10→5, 11-50→30, 51-200→125, 201-500→350, 500+→750)
- revenueEstimateM: range midpoint in millions (Under $1M→0.5, $1M-$5M→3, $5M-$20M→12.5, $20M-$100M→50, $100M+→150)

SECTION 2 — pain_points (3–6 items)
Input contains processes[]: an array of processes the exec explicitly identified.
- Each process in processes[] becomes a pain_point with source="user_stated", confidence="high"
- Use the process name as the title. Write a 1-sentence description of the operational impact.
- Add 1–2 industry-typical inferred pain points if needed to reach 3 items: source="inferred", confidence="medium"

SECTION 3 — workflows (exactly 4)
Map the top 4 processes from processes[] to workflows (or all of them if fewer than 4 exist, filling remaining with inferred).
Processes with volume_per_month and time_per_item set are the most data-rich — prioritize them.
Each: name (match the process name exactly), function (e.g. "Sales Operations"), owner (job title matching the department), whyItMatters, agentName (catchy AI agent name), expectedOutcome (specific operational KPI, no revenue claims), sourceType ("user_stated" or "inferred")

Return valid JSON only. No commentary.

You are generating numeric ROI assumptions for an AI automation report.

Input fields: company_profile, workflows[] (exactly 4 from Company Analyst), processes[] (structured list from the executive), selectedCurrency.

CURRENCY: Parse from selectedCurrency (format "CODE – Name (symbol)"). Use exact GCC symbols: SAR→"ر.س", AED→"د.إ", QAR→"ر.ق", KWD→"د.ك", BHD→"BD", OMR→"ر.ع.". If blank, infer from country.

VOLUME & TIME ANCHORING (critical — these are hard anchors):
Input contains processes[]: each has volume_per_month and time_per_item from the executive directly.
For each workflow in workflows[], find the matching process in processes[] by name.
If a match exists and volume_per_month is set, use it as the monthlyVolume anchor:
- "Under 30 per month"   → 15–25
- "30–100 per month"     → 40–85
- "100–300 per month"    → 110–250
- "Over 300 per month"   → 300–500
If time_per_item is set, use it as minutesPerItemBefore:
- "Under 30 minutes" → 20–28
- "30–60 minutes"    → 35–55
- "1–2 hours"        → 65–110
- "Over 2 hours"     → 130–180
If no match or value is null: use conservative industry benchmark (lower end of range for company size).

AUTOMATION TIME REDUCTION TARGETS — use these to set minutesPerItemAfter:
AI automation eliminates the manual execution portion of a task. The human's remaining role is to review,
approve, and submit — not redo the work. Apply these reduction targets to minutesPerItemBefore:
- Document generation (proposals, quotes, contracts, reports): 70–85% reduction
- Data entry / CRM updates / system logging: 80–92% reduction
- Status reporting / dashboards / summaries: 72–85% reduction
- Client onboarding coordination / scheduling: 55–72% reduction
- Email drafting / follow-ups / communications: 70–82% reduction
- Invoice / financial reconciliation: 65–80% reduction
Example: if a proposal takes 90 min before, minutesPerItemAfter should be 90 × (1 - 0.75) = ~22 min.
Do NOT set minutesPerItemAfter above 40% of minutesPerItemBefore — AI automation always delivers substantial gains.

VOLUME CEILING: No single workflow monthlyVolume may exceed employees × 2.

COSTS:
- implementationCost: MUST equal 6–10× estimated monthly labor savings. Enforces a 6–10 month payback.
- monthlyToolingCost: recurring platform/license fees.

LABOR:
- fullyLoadedHourlyCost: blended rate for this country and industry.
- workWeeksPerYear: 48 for GCC/Egypt, 50 for US/EU.

realizationFactor: 0.70–0.85. LyRise's structured onboarding ensures adoption; do not go below 0.70.
profitMultiplier: 1.5–4.0. Cap at 3.5 for companies under 500 employees.

workflowAssumptions (exactly 4, names must match workflows input):
- minutesPerItemAfter: apply the AUTOMATION TIME REDUCTION TARGETS above. Must be ≤ 40% of minutesPerItemBefore.
- exceptionRate: 0.05–0.20, exceptionMinutes, adoption_low/base/high.
- rationale: 1 sentence citing employee count, industry norms, and why volume is realistic.

SANITY CHECK — annual labor savings = sum(volume × timeSaved/60 × rate × realization × 12):
- Under 200 employees: $60K–$300K total.
- 200–2000 employees: $150K–$700K total.
Scale down volumes only if the upper bound is substantially exceeded.

Return valid JSON only.

values.content:
You write copy for a client-facing AI automation ROI report.

CRITICAL — NUMBER DISCIPLINE:
All financial figures are pre-computed and provided in the `figures` field.
You MUST copy these strings verbatim. Do NOT compute, round, or substitute any numbers.
- Hours returned per month: figures.totalMonthlyHours
- Hours returned per year: figures.totalAnnualHours
- Operational Dividend: figures.operationalDividend12mo
- Profit Uplift: figures.profitUplift12mo
- Total Financial Gain: figures.totalFinancialGain12mo

OUTPUT 4 SECTIONS:

1. executiveSummary (200–280 words, exactly 4 paragraphs)

Para 1 — Context (2–3 sentences): Name the company, industry, approximate size, and 1–2 of their stated challenges using their EXACT words from pain_points where source="user_stated". Set the scene. NO financial figures in this paragraph.

Para 2 — Workflows (2–3 sentences): Name the 4 workflows and explain why each addresses their specific challenges. Connect directly to what they told us. NO financial figures in this paragraph.

Para 3 — Human Impact (2–3 sentences): Translate the opportunity into human terms. Use EXACTLY figures.statFTE as the FTE equivalent (e.g. "the equivalent of figures.statFTE full-time contributors"). What does this mean for the people doing the work? Use language around: time freed for higher-value work, reduced repetitive burden, team capacity redirected to clients. NO financial figures in this paragraph.

Para 4 — Opportunity + CTA (2 sentences): State EXACTLY figures.totalFinancialGain12mo as the total 12-month financial gain. Acknowledge estimates are directional. Invite a 30-min validation call.

CRITICAL: ONLY Para 4 may contain any financial figure. Paras 1–3 are purely narrative.
Use "we" (LyRise), never "I". No hype. No generic phrases.

2. cta_paragraph (2–3 sentences)
Use EXACTLY figures.operationalDividend12mo and figures.totalMonthlyHours hrs. Invite a 30-min discovery call.

3. profit_levers (exactly 3 items in this order)
The 3 profits MUST sum to EXACTLY profitUplift12mo (the raw integer, provided separately).

CRITICAL for all levers:
- baseline_data: 1 factual sentence grounded in the company's workflows (use process names, volumes, or hours from figures.workflowLines). No invented numbers.
- assumption: 1 concrete sentence on what the automation enables. Use percentages and hours. No invented business context.
- rationale: 1 plain business sentence on WHY this creates value. Write it the way a CFO would say it in a board meeting. NEVER mention seasons, holidays, named events, or external market conditions. Do not use the word "Christmas", "seasonal", or any time-specific references. The sentence should read: "[Outcome] because [direct operational reason]."

Lever 1 — Revenue Throughput:
  baseline_data: reference the proposal or highest-value workflow volume and current time cost (from figures.workflowLines).
  assumption: state the % of freed time redirected to pipeline activity and the estimated deal-value uplift.
  CAP profit at min(derived, profitUplift12mo × 0.45). Set lever1 = capped value.

Lever 2 — Error Reduction:
  baseline_data: reference the CRM, data entry, or reporting workflow — state current volume and error-prone nature.
  assumption: state the % reduction in errors/rework and the hours saved per month.
  CAP profit at min(derived, profitUplift12mo × 0.30). Set lever2 = capped value.

Lever 3 — Strategic Capacity:
  Set lever3 = profitUplift12mo - lever1 - lever2. Always positive.
  baseline_data: state what % of senior/skilled staff time currently goes to administrative or low-value tasks (cite a specific number from the workflows, e.g. "X hrs/week across Y roles").
  assumption: state how many hrs/month are redirected to strategic or billable work, and the direct financial impact.

Each lever fields:
- lever_name: short label
- baseline_data: 1 sentence on the current inefficiency with a specific number
- assumption: 1 sentence on what automation enables, with a specific percentage
- rationale: 1 plain business sentence on why this lever creates value — no formulas, no math, no equals signs
- profit: positive integer as string, no symbols or commas (e.g. "86401")

4. risks_mitigations (3–4 items)
Implementation risks ONLY — not the client's pain points.
Risk 1: adoption variance (cite employee count). Risk 2: data/integration complexity for their industry.
Risk 3: volume calibration (cite a specific workflow volume). Risk 4 (optional): technical risk for one named workflow.
Each: risk (1 sentence), mitigation (1 sentence referencing LyRise methodology).

Return valid JSON only.

About LyRise

We map critical business processes, quantify true run-costs, and deploy targeted automations and AI agents to return time to teams and unlock measurable financial gains.

Recognising your deep expertise in {{ $json.roi_data.industry || 'your industry' }}, this report models how AI can be applied to the core processes that power your business.

    {{ $json.display.scopeListHTML }}

  • Operational Dividend{{ $json.display.od12 }}

  • Total Financial Gain{{ $json.display.tf12 }}

All assumptions should be validated with {{ $json.roi_data.company }} stakeholders before implementation decisions are made.

Roadmap — 8 to 10 Weeks to Measurable Gains

Weeks 1-2
Rapid Discovery & Validation
Map live workflows, validate volumes and task times, confirm automation priorities.
Weeks 3-6
Pilot Build & Testing
Deploy AI agents for the top-priority workflow. Integrate with existing systems and run parallel testing.
Weeks 7-8
Controlled Go-Live
Launch with real data. Monitor exceptions, adoption rates, and time savings against predictions.
Weeks 9-10
ROI Validation & Expansion
Confirm realised savings vs. model. Present results to leadership and plan remaining rollout.

Next Steps & Sign-Off Checklist

✓Approve pilot workflow to begin in Week 3 ✓Schedule validation & process-mapping session
